document.title: M1 Lattice: Transport Specification (Epoch 2)
document.purpose: Defines how M1 entities and aspects are represented for storage and exchange.

core_principle: An M1 transport unit is a structured collection of entity contributions indexed by entity identifier. Each contribution may contain aspects and transport-level tombstone instructions. Entities are represented solely by their identifiers.
design_principles: Entities are identified solely by their identifier.
Aspects carry entity meaning and structure.
Transport-level instructions are distinct from aspects.
Transport structure is optimized for convenience of reading and writing.
Transport does not prescribe in-memory representation.
Transport does not define merging or conflict resolution.
Partial knowledge is valid and expected.

scope.description: The transport specification defines how entities and aspects are serialized for storage and exchange. It does not define runtime behavior such as merging, conflict resolution, or query semantics.

file_format.description: M1 transport units are typically stored as files with the '.m1' extension.
rules: The '.m1' extension designates a file as containing M1 Lattice transport data.
Files with the '.m1' extension are expected to conform to this transport specification.
Applications may associate the '.m1' extension with tools for viewing, editing, or processing M1 data.
The use of the '.m1' extension is recommended but not required; transport units may also be embedded in other formats or systems.

minimal_compliance: Recognizes transport-level entity and aspect tombstones
Parses transport structure into entity identifiers, associated aspects, and transport instructions
Reads and exposes table mappings if present

transport_unit.description: A transport unit is a file or data structure containing a set of entities and their associated aspects. It represents a partial contribution to the M1 Lattice. See the softspec schema 'transport_unit' for the formal structure.
rules: All top-level sections are optional except the 'm1' header.
The m1 header must include an id identifying the transport unit itself.
The m1 header may include a series_id identifying a persistent document-series lineage.
The m1 header may include a created timestamp indicating when the document series was created.
The m1 header must include a timestamp indicating the current emission time of the transport unit.
A transport unit may contain any subset of entities and aspects.
Multiple transport units may contribute data about the same entity.
Transport units are not required to be complete or self-contained.

entity_representation.description: Entities are represented implicitly by their presence as keys in the 'entities' map or by reference within aspect contents.
rules: An entity is represented in a transport unit if its identifier appears as a key in the 'entities' map.
An entity entry may contribute aspects, aspect tombstones, or both, provided that no aspect is both contributed and tombstoned; alternatively, it may contribute a whole-entity tombstone.
Representational presence in one transport unit does not by itself determine whether the entity is visible in a layered interpretation.
An entity may be referenced without being explicitly represented.
An entity has no intrinsic structure beyond its identifier.

aspect_representation.description: Aspects are represented as key-value pairs within an entity entry.
rules: Each aspect is identified by its key within an entity entry.
An aspect identifier MUST be an entity-id.
An aspect identifier is RECOMMENDED to be a Tag URI.
The reserved keys 'tombstone' and 'tombstones' are transport instructions and are not aspect identifiers.
The value associated with an aspect identifier is the aspect content.
Aspect content may be any valid JSON value, including null.
The expected type, structure, and interpretation of aspect content are determined by the aspect identifier.
The presence of an aspect identifier means that the aspect is present, regardless of its content value.
Multiple aspects may be associated with a single entity.

tombstone_representation.description: Explicit absence in a layered interpretation is represented by reserved transport keys within an entity entry. Tombstones are transport instructions and are not aspects.
rules: An entity entry is not required to contain either 'tombstone' or 'tombstones'.
The reserved key 'tombstone' expresses a whole-entity tombstone.
The value of 'tombstone' MUST be true.
The reserved key 'tombstones' expresses aspect-level tombstones.
The value of 'tombstones' MUST be an array of aspect identifiers.
Each value in the 'tombstones' array MUST be an aspect identifier.
An entity or aspect omitted from a transport makes no assertion of absence.
A tombstone is an explicit contribution and is not equivalent to omission.
A null aspect value is an ordinary present aspect value and does not express a tombstone.
References to tombstoned or omitted entities MAY remain in the same or other transport units.
constraints: The keys 'tombstone' and 'tombstones' MUST NOT both appear in the same entity entry.
An entity entry containing 'tombstone': true MUST NOT contain aspect contributions.
An aspect identifier MUST NOT appear both as an aspect contribution and in the 'tombstones' array of the same entity entry.

containment.description: A transport unit represents a partial, non-authoritative contribution to the global graph.
rules: Entities may reference identifiers not present in the same transport unit.
A transport unit does not imply completeness.
Multiple transport units may describe the same entity.

composition.description: Multiple transport units may overlap, contributing data about the same entities. How overlapping contributions are combined is the responsibility of the consuming application, not the specification.
rules: Entities with the same identifier refer to the same logical entity.
Multiple transport units may provide aspects for the same entity.
Tables from different transport units may map the same identifier to different locations.
No transport unit is considered authoritative over another.

identity_resolution.description: Identity resolution governs how references from multiple transport units are unified.
rules: The entity-id, expressed as either a UUID or Tag URI, is the authoritative identifier.
UUIDs MUST use the standard hyphenated lowercase textual form.
Tag URIs MUST be preserved exactly and compared using exact string equality.
Two identical entity-id strings of the same identifier form identify the same logical entity.
Different Tag URI strings MUST NOT be unified through canonicalization, normalization, case-folding, decoding, re-encoding, or other rewriting.
If the same entity-id appears to refer to distinct things, this is a structural problem and SHOULD be reported.
Implementations must resolve entity-id values to their corresponding entities when possible.

merge_rules.description: Merging behavior is not defined by this specification. How overlapping contributions are combined, prioritized, or reconciled is the responsibility of consuming applications. See m1lattice.merging.guide.v2 for exploratory guidance.
notes: Applications may accumulate, select, annotate, partition, quarantine, or refuse conflicting values.
A minimal baseline: treat identical values for the same entity and aspect as one; treat differing values as conflicts to be resolved by the application.
No merge strategy is mandated by M1.

loading_model.description: Defines the conceptual behavior of loading M1 transport units into memory.
rules: Loading produces a merged, identity-indexed view of the data.
Entities are retrievable by their identifier.
Aspects are accessible by entity identifier and aspect identifier.
Tables provide resolution context but do not alter the ontology.
Implementations are not required to use a specific in-memory structure.

mutation_model.description: Defines how changes may be represented in transport units.
rules: Transport units may represent incremental additions to the graph.
Updates may be expressed by providing new aspect values for existing entities.
A null aspect value is an ordinary present aspect value.
Aspect-level absence in a layered interpretation may be expressed with the reserved 'tombstones' transport key.
Whole-entity absence in a layered interpretation may be expressed with the reserved 'tombstone' transport key.
Physical omission from a rewritten transport is a persistence decision and does not itself assert absence.
Append-only and layered models are supported but not required.
The transport format does not prescribe a specific persistence strategy.

tables.description: Tables provide a mapping from identifiers to resource locations. They are not part of the M1 ontology.
rules: Tables are optional.
Tables are environment-specific.
Tables may differ across systems without affecting identity.
Transport does not define how table entries are resolved.

boundaries.description: Defines how transport units relate to datasets and environments.
rules: Transport units do not define dataset boundaries.
Datasets are defined by the set of transport units loaded by a system.
The same transport unit may participate in multiple datasets.
Systems must not assume completeness of any dataset.

interoperability.description: Defines how systems exchange and interpret transport units.
rules: Systems must recognize the 'm1' header and its version field.
Systems must support partial data and incremental loading.
Systems must interoperate via shared identity conventions.
Systems conforming to version 3.0 must distinguish aspects from reserved transport instructions.
No shared schema is required beyond m1lattice.spec.core.v3 and this transport specification.

m1_transport_unit.title: M1 Transport Unit
m1_transport_unit.description: A transport unit is a file or data structure containing entity contributions composed of aspects and optional transport-level tombstone instructions.

m1.description: Header identifying the file as M1 transport data.

id.description: UUID identifying this transport unit itself. This identifier is bound to this specific emitted transport artifact and must not be reused for changed content.

series_id.description: Optional UUID identifying the persistent document series or lineage to which this emitted transport unit belongs.

version.description: Specifies the version of the M1 transport format.

created.description: Optional timestamp indicating when the document series was originally created.

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when this transport unit was emitted. This value is updated whenever the transport unit is written. A changed re-emission must receive both a new id and a new timestamp.

author.description: A human-readable indication of who created or contributed this transport unit. No formal structure or identifier is required.

title.description: A human-readable title for the transport unit, suitable for display in file browsers or user interfaces.

description.description: A textual description of the transport unit, providing context about its contents or purpose.

hook.description: A concise, attention-directing phrase intended to quickly convey the significance or purpose of the transport unit.

entities.description: A mapping from entity identifiers to entity contributions.

value_type.description: An entity contribution containing aspect values or transport-level tombstone instructions.

tombstones.description: Aspect identifiers explicitly tombstoned by this contribution.

value_type.description: Aspect content. May be any valid JSON value. Its expected type, structure, and interpretation are determined by the aspect identifier.

constraints: An entity entry may contain 'tombstone', 'tombstones', or neither, but MUST NOT contain both.
An entity entry containing 'tombstone': true MUST NOT contain aspect contributions.
An aspect identifier MUST NOT appear both as an aspect contribution and in the 'tombstones' array of the same entity entry.

table.description: Optional mapping of identifiers to resource locations. Structure as defined in m1lattice.spec.core.v3, schema 'table'.
